Anthony), baptised 8 Feb., 1634-5, created a baronet 29 Jan., 1660, licenced 30 May, 1662, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of Sir Henry Massingberd, of Bratoft Hall, co. Lincoln, bart.; died 30 June, 1686; father of Laurence 1686. See Fasti, ii. 220; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Fairfax, Henry, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653, B.A. 7 Feb., 1656-7, M.A. 21 June, 1659 (incorporated at Cambridge 1662); fellow of Magdalen Coll. 1659-1687 and 1688, B.D. 26 April, 1666, D.D. 10 March, 1680-1 (6s. Charles, of Menston, Yorks), rector of Tubney, Berks, 1683, dean of Norwich 1689, until his death 10 May, 1702; brother of Thomas 1650. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Fasti, ii. 374; Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 239; & D.N.B.

Welman, Simon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653; demy Magdalen Coll. 1655-8, B.A. 13 Oct., 1656, fellow 1658-61, M.A. 2 June, 1659; incorporated D.Med. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1686 (2s. Simon, of Taunton, M.P.); fellow college of physicians 1687; died 1707. See Bloxam, v. 220; & Munk's Roll, i. 452.

Terry, Robert, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653; B.A. from New Inn Hall 13 Nov., 1656, M.A. 25 June, 1659; rector of Tallaton, Devon, 1662, licenced 10 July, 1663, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of William Venner, of Kirton. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Edmonds, Gideon, "serv." Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653; B.A. 6 Feb., 1656-7, M.A. 3 July, 1660, rector of St. Mary Arches in Exeter 1663, and of Milton Damerell, Devon, 1664; another of these names rector of Clyst St. George, Devon, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bastard, William, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653; Sir William, of Gerston, Devon, knighted at Whitehall 10 Aug., 1677, M.P. Beeralston 1678-9, 1679-81 (son of William, of Duloe, M.P. Devon 1654-5), buried 2 July, 1690. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Hearne, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653, B.A. 6 Feb., 1656-7, fellow 1657-62 and 1663-90, M.A. 21 June, 1659, proctor 1664, B.D. 15 June, 1669, D.D. 4 March, 1686-7, rector of St. Anne's, Soho, 1686-1704. See Boase, 73; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Chudleigh, (Sir) George, Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4 (as arm.), 3rd bart.; admitted to the Inner Temple 1656, as of Ashton, Devon, esq., (son of Sir George), died in 1719, brother of John, 1657, and of George 1654. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hayter, George, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653, B.A. 28 May, 1657; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 1661, rector of Chagford, Devon, 1662, and of St. Ewe, Cornwall, 1672; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Harris, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653; possibly student of Inner Temple 1653, as son and heir of John, of Plymouth, Devon, gent.; perhaps father of John 1678. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Rose, William, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653, B.A. 13 Dec., 1656, M.A. 2 June, 1659; one of these names rector of Poxwell AprilNov., 1667, and of Swanage, Dorset, 1667-90. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Northcote, Samuel, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653; B.A. 6 Feb., 1656-7, vicar of Deddington, Oxon, 1664, vicar of West Alwington 1668, and rector of Otterham, Devon, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gidleigh, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4, B.A. 10 Oct., 1657, M.A. 21 June, 1660; lived at Exeter upon his own estate; settled at Great Marlow, Bucks, where he died. See Calamy, ii. 109.

Hele, Nicholas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653, B.A. 5 March, 1656-7; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 24 June, 1659; B.Med. from Exeter Coll. 18 Dec., 1665, D.Med. 7 July, 1669; father of the next-named.

Lobb, Richard, arm. fil. nat. max. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653; perhaps M.P. St. Michael, Cornwall, 1659, and father of the minister of Fetter Lane chapel. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Towns(h)end, Clement, "serv." Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653, B.A. 28 May, 1657 (as Townson), M.A. 11 July, 1660, D.Med. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1669, incorporated 13 July, 1669.
Manly, Robert, "serv." Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653; perhaps rector of Moreton Hampstead 1663, of Powderham 1672, and vicar of Halberton (all Devon), 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knight, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653, B.A. 15 Oct., 1656; M.A. from New Coll. 2 June, 1659, rector of Little Hempston, Devon, until ejected 1662. See Calamy, ii. 40.
Avery, Robert, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653; admitted to Gray's Inn 1657, as son and heir apparent of Robert Avery, of Witheridge, Devon, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Tredinham, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4; student of Middle Temple 1653, as son and heir of John, of Filley, Cornwall, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wood, Ezekiel, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653, B.A. 14 May, 1657, M.A. 3 May, 1660; rector of Stowford 1661, and of Lifton, (both) Devon, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tuckfield, Roger, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 March, 1653, B.A. 13 Oct., 1656; student of Inner Temple 1653, as son of Roger, of Devon, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Budd, James, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4; B.A. 10 Oct., 1657, M.A. 3 July, 1660, rector of West Horsley, Surrey, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dene, Elijah, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 March, 1653 (called Elisha in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 15 Oct., 1656 (as Elias), of Clist Hedon, Devon, father of the next named.
Gregory, George, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653, B.A. 13 Dec., 1656, rector of Charles, Devon, 1664; father of Anthony 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wills, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653; (s. John 1608), rector of St. Endellion, Cornwall, 1661; brother of Jonathan. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Carew, George, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653, of Gray's Inn 1653, as son and heir of George, late of Hitcham, Suffolk, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Preston, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653; perhaps rector of St. Mary Tavy, Devon, 1660, and father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lee, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653; B.A. from New Coll. 28 May, 1657; perhaps rector of North Stoke, Somerset, 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Drake, Joseph, 'Baronetti fil.' Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653, (son of Sir Francis, 1st baronet), buried at Buckland Monachorum, Devon, 12 Oct., 1708.
Trenhail, Reginald, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1653; vicar of Madron, Cornwall, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dalgardno, Robert, 'ser.' Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4, B.A. 28 May, 1657, M.A. 3 July, 1660.
Cholwich, Richard, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653, called Colwich in Mat. Reg.
Pidsley, Clement, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653, B.A. 15 Oct., 1656.
Bouchier, Tobias, 'ser.' Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653. See Calamy, i. 349.
Seward, Richard (Seaward), gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653.
Jones, George (Johns), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653.
Lethbridge, William, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1653.
Backaller, Henry, pleb. Exeter Coll, matric. 17 March, 1653-4.
Culling, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653.
Balliman, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 July, 1653.
Henley, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1653-4.
Palmer, William, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 June, 1653.

Stanhope, Alexander, comitis fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; of Hartshorn, co. Derby, student of Inner Temple 1656 (as son of Philip, earl of Chesterfield), gentleman usher to the Queen, envoy extraordinary to Spain 1689-1706; died 20 Sept., 1707; father of James 1688, half-brother of Charles 1632. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Quicke, John, 'ser.' Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 10 Oct., 1657, (perhaps) student of Lincoln's Inn 1651 (as son and heir of John, of Morchard, Devon, esq.), silenced for nonconformity 1662, had a congregation in London 1694, died 29 April, 1706, aged 70. See Ath. iv. 493; Fasti, ii. 198; Burrows, 507; & Calamy, ii. 9.

Hore, William, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 10 Oct., 1657, M.A. 13 July, 1660, incorporated at Cambridge 1683, and LL.D. from Trinity Hall 1683, chaplain in ordinary to the king, canon of Heytesbury 1674, and of Worcester 1676, reector of Coulsdon, Surrey, 1678. See Fasti, ii. 381; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Page, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654, B.A. 9 Feb., 1657-8, M.A. 7 Aug., 1660; one of these names vicar of Ledbury 1664, rector of Coddington 1669, and of Colwall (all co. Hereford), 1673; perhaps instituted a second time to Coddington 1681-97. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Meynell, Thomas (Maynell), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 11 March, 1657-8; M.A. from Hart Hall 7 Aug., 1660, rector of South Kilworth, co. Leicester, 1662, and of Kirk Langley, co. Derby, 1662, canon of Lichfield 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Russell, John, "ser.," Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 15 Jan., 1657-8, M.A. 13 July, 1660; vicar of South Brent 1668-71, and East Brent, Somerset, 1670, until his death in 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hancocke, Jonathan, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1660, incorporated at Cambridge (as M.A.) 1663, vicar of Beddington, Sussex, 1664. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Atkey, Alexander, 'serv.' Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 15 Jan., 1657-8; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 30 Oct., 1660, vicar of Ilfracombe 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bawden, Thomas, 'serv.' Exeter Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654; B.A. 11 June, 1658, vicar of Padstow, Cornwall, 1670, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rowe, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; B.A. 15 Jan., 1657-8, M.A. 3 Oct., 1660; perhaps vicar of Coleridge, Devon, 1661-1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Browne, Peter, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; B.A. 15 Jan., 1657-8, rector of Langley, Kent, 1662, vicar of Chart 1664. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Roberts, Herbert, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654; student of Gray's Inn 1657, as y.s. of John, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Bastard, Richard, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, one of these names vicar of Northam, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Shapley, James, cler fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654; vicar of West Leigh, Devon, 1668. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wauchop, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. 15 Jan., 1657-8; M.A. from New Inn Hall 4 June, 1662.
Grayle, Thomas (Graile), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654, B.A. April, 1658.
Chapman, Richard, 'ser.' Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654.
Se(a)rle, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654.
Dennis, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 July, 1654.
Taylor, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1654.

Harvey, Gideon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655; of St. Mary Aldermary, London, he was licenced 19 Dec., 1664 (then a doctor of physic), to marry Elizabeth Leigh, of St. Matthew's, Friday Street, London, spinster; physician in ordinary to Charles II. when in exile, fellow college of physicians, at the Hague, physician to the English army in Flanders; perhaps D.Med. at Leyden. See Ath. iv. 494; Munk's Roll, ii. 11; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & D.N.B.

Verman, George, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655, B.A. 1660, M.A. 21 Jan., 1662-3; (incorporated at Cambridge 1664), proctor 1672, B.D. 1674, fellow 1657, until his death 29 March, 1718, aged 83; buried in the college chapel; will at Oxford, proved 24 April, 1718; perhaps rector of Mells, Somerset, 1693. See Ath. i. p. lxxii; Boase, 73; Gutch, i. 121; & Rawl. vi. 109.

Sowton, Stephen, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655, B.A. 17 Jan., 1658-9, M.A. 1662; rector of Staplehurst, Kent, 1662; licenced 30 Nov., 1668, to marry Anne, daughter of John Woodall, rector of Iden, Sussex; rector of Folkton, Yorks, 1674. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, etc.

Galpine, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. from New Inn Hall 12 Oct., 1658, served the cure of Ashpriors, Somerset, ejected 1662 for nonconformity; died at Totnes Sept., 1698. See Calamy, iii. 166.

Huish, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655, B.A. 12 Oct., 1658, M.A. 14 June, 1661; perhaps rector of Cucklington, Oxon, 1660-2, and of North Petherton, Somerset, 1662-77. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rands, Richard, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. 12 Feb., 1658-9, M.A. 11 April, 1662, rector of Bethersden, and of Smarden, Kent, 1668-9, and of Turvey, Beds, 1669-99. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Evans, Henry, cler fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655; B.A. from New Inn Hall. 12 Oct., 1658, M.A. 15 June, 1661; one of these names vicar of East Chudleigh, Devon, 1664. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Izacke, Samuel, gent. (Isaac in M.R.). Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655, B.A. 4 Nov., 1658; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1661; B. & D. Med. from Exeter Coll. 1675; father of Robert and Samuel next-named.
Gauden, Samuel, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655, B.A. 19 Feb., 1657-8, student of Lincoln's Inn 1657, as son and heir of Dennis of Clapham, Surrey, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Revell, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655; B.A. 12 Oct., 1658, M.A. 14 June, 1661, rector of Ermington, Devon, 1672; brother of the last-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Arundell, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655; John, eldest son of John, of Trubhall, Cornwall, Esq., admitted to the Middle Temple 1657. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Graves, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; student of Lincoln's Inn 1655, as son and heir of Richard, of Richmond, Surrey, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gilbert, Samuel, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655, a student of Inner Temple 1657, as of Bridgerule, Cornwall; see Richard 1695. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Horwell, Peirce, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 31 May, 1655; B.A. 17 March, 1658-9; vicar of Maristow, Devon, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jeffreys, Edward, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655, B.A. 12 Feb., 1658-9; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 1661.
Goswell, Edward, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 July, 1655; B.A. from New Inn Hall 27 April, 1659.
Flavell, Gregory, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655, called George Flavill in Mat. Reg.
Dickes, John, 'cler. fil.' Exeter Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1655; B.A. 4 June, 1659.
Atwood, Henry, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. Michaelmas term 1655.
Lee, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 March, 1655.

Paynter, William, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, fellow 1657-88, B.A. 1660, M.A. 21 Jan., 1662-3 (incorporated at Cambridge 1664), B.D. 1674, D.D. 1695, rector of his college 1690-1715, vice-chancellor 1698-1700 (s. William, of Antron in Sithney, Cornwall); born at Trelissick; baptised at St. Erth 7 Dec., 1637, rector of Wotton, Northants, 1686 (as Paynter alias Cambourne), until his death there 18 Feb., 1716; admon. at Oxford 2 April, 1716. See Boase, 73.

Pett, Phineas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, B.A. 28 Feb., 1659-60, M.A. 1662, vicar of Totnes, Devon, Sept., 1669, licenced 13 Aug., 1669, to marry Hester, daughter of Robert Rogers, of Wappenham, Northants, clerk; vicar of Paignton, Devon, 1674, until his death 1681. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Harris, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, B.A. 2 June, 1659, M.A. 11 April, 1662; chaplain-fellow by the royal commissioners 1660-6; one of these names vicar of St. Issey, Cornwall, 1666, and of St. Clether 1672; buried at St. Issey 27 April, 1696. See Humphrey 1662 and Joseph 1670. See Boase, 74; and Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Woollnough, Henry, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; student of Middle Temple, 1661 (as o.s. Joshua, of city of London, gent.), M.A. Cambridge 1663, rector of Shelley, Essex, 1663; licenced 3 Nov., 1663, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of John Platt, of Godalming, Surrey, clerk. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Credeford, George, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664, incorp. at Cambridge 1668, rector of Torbrian, Devon, 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: licenced (v.g.) 31 Dec., 1688, to marry Amy Jess, of Torbrian, Devon, spinster, aged 30.]

Allen, Benjamin, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1660, clerk of Magdalen College 1659-61, rector of Lamorran, Cornwall, 1660, of Binstead, Isle of Wight, 1662, of St. Michael, Penkevill, Cornwall, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Bloxam, ii. 72.

Morth, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, student of Gray's Inn 1657, and of Middle Temple 1659 (as son and heir of Edward, of Talland, Cornwall, esq.), high sheriff 1696; father of the last-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Onslow, John, equitis fil. nat. min. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, B.A. 12 Feb., 1658-9; possibly a student of Inner Temple 1662, as of West Clandon, Surrey, gent. (s. Sir Richard); died unmarried. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Bluett, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, student of Lincoln's Inn 1656, as son and heir of Francis, late of Holcombe Rogus, Devon, esq., died 30 Sept., 1700, buried 16th, at Holcombe. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Gooddall, George, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, fellow 1658-89, B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664; (incorporated at Cambridge 1668), B.D. 1674, rector of Padworth, Berks, 1683. See Boase, 73; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Veryard, Andrew, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; vicar of St. Decuman, Somerset, 1663-96, and perhaps rector of Hazlebury, Wilts, 1683-1712; father of the next, and of Ellis. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Chambre, George, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, possibly of Lincoln's Inn 1663 (as son and heir of Francis, of Loppington, Salop, esq.), died in 1661, aged 53. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Brewer, William (Bruer), pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1656-7; one of these names vicar of Hougham St. Laurence, Kent, 1675, rector of Charlton, Kent, 1690. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Radford, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; B.A. from Hart Hall 1660, rector of West Itchenor, Sussex, 1676, and vicar of Butleigh, Somerset, 1677. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Shirley, Drew, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; perhaps a brother of Anthony 1642, and son of Thomas by his wife Elizabeth, daughter of Drew Stapley, of London. See Berry's Sussex, 172.
Upton, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, B.A. 27 Jan., 1658-9; of Lupton, Devon (s. Arthur 1631), M.P. Clifton-Dartmouth-Hardness, 1678-81. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Hillersdon, Richard, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; of Membland, Devon, perhaps M.P. Plympton 1678-9, father of the next-named. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Hawkey, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, a student of Middle Temple 1656, as son and heir of Thomas of Lanlawen, Cornwall, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Martin, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; B.A. from Christ Church 1659. M.A. 1662; perhaps rector of Sampford Orcas, Somerset, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rose, Richard, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; student of Lincoln's Inn 1659, as 4s. Richard, of Wootton Fitzwarren, Dorset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Upcott, Ozias, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, fellow 1659-64, B.A. 1662; rector of Honiton, Devon, 1663, until his death 6 Feb., 1698-9. See Boase, 74.
Hall, Charles, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from New Inn Hall 16 March, 1659-60, vicar of Fremington, Devon, 1661, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Coke, Christopher, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, of Lincoln's Inn 1656, as 2nd son of John Coke, of Tregasaw, Cornwall. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Oxenbridge, Daniel, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; clerk of Magdalen Coll. 1657-60, B.A. 29 Feb., 1659-60. See Bloxam, ii. 71; & Burrows, 502.
Watts, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1660; perhaps rector of Romansleigh, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Trewinnard, James, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; vicar of Breage 1662, and rector of Mawnam, Cornwall, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wills, William, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, B.A. 1659; one of these names vicar of Horley, Surrey, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Parsons, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656, B.A. 1665; perhaps vicar of Northover, Somerset, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sanford, Christopher, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7, B.A. 1660; rector of Folke, Dorset, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Yeo, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, B.A. 1659; rector of Wear Gifford. Devon, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Barbor, William (or Bareboe), pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7, B.A. 1660. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ingram, Anthony, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 1660, called "Engram" in Mat. Reg.
Hele, George, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; perhaps of Bradridge, Cornwall, and father of Francis 1683.
Pulsforde, William, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 28 Feb., 1659-60.
Lampen, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656; B.A. from Hart Hall 25 Feb., 1660-1.
Pett, Peter, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Thompson, Henry, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; B.A. from New Inn Hall 1660.
Collibeare, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663.
Marshall, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1660.
Milles, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1660.
Clarke, Robert, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656; B.A. 1659.
Baker, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1656-7; B.A. 1660.
Bond, Thomas, 'serv.' Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7.
Josling, Gideon, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656.
Whiting, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656.
Strode, George, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656.
Symons, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7.
Adams, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1656-7.
Gealard, Owen, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656.
Hooke, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 29 March, 1656-7.
Baron, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 July, 1656.

Hole, Matthew, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661, fellow 1663-89 and 1716-30, M.A. 1664, B.D. 1674, D.D. 1716, rector of his college 1715-30, vicar of Bishops Lavington, Wilts, 1673-4, of Stogursey, Somerset, 1689, canon of Wells 1687, rector of Fiddington, Somerset, 1709; died 19 July, 1730, aged 90; buried in the college chapel; will at Oxford 4 May, 1730. See Fasti, ii. 344; Rawl. i. 140, xx. 267; Boase, 75; Gutch, i. 120; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.

Rost, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663; licenced 1 Aug., 1663, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of Nathaniel Taylor, of Cassington, Oxon, gent., vicar of Winsham, Somerset, 1667-75, rector of Offwell 1668, and of Gittisham, Devon, 1681, until his death in 1713. See Rawl. v. 24; Foster's Index Eccl.; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Hatsell, (Sir) Henry, Exeter Coll. matric. 15 June, 1657, as arm., B.A. 4 Feb., 1659-60; bar.-atlaw, Middle Temple, 1667 (as eldest son of Henry, of Saltram, Devon, esq.), serjeant-at-law 1 May, 1689, knighted 2 Dec., 1697, baron of court of exchequer 1697-1702; died April, 1714. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & D.N.B.

Reynolds, John (Reinolds), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661; M.A. from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1676 (s. Joshua); canon of Exeter 1679, vicar of St. Thomas, near Exeter, and headmaster of Exeter grammar school; died 1692; father of John 1693, of Joshua 1691, and of Samuel.

Coulding, Edward, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, of the Middle Temple 28 Nov., 1657, as eldest son of John, of Hill Court, co. Worcester, esq.; licence 19 July, 1665 (then a widower) to marry Dame Katherine Adler, of St. Martin-in-the-Fields, Middlesex, widow. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Pen, Nathaniel, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 Dec., 1657 ('ser.'), B.A. 1661, M.A. 19 Jan., 1663-4 (as Penn), vicar of Lewknor 1663, rector of Whitfield, Oxon, 1666; licenced 27 July, 1666, to marry Margaret Wentworth, widow. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Ceely, John (gent. 1658, arm. 1660). Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8; B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663, vicar of Paignton, Devon, 1670, canon of Exeter 1674, rector of Clyst St. Mary, Devon, 1682, perhaps brother of William 1659. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Matthew(es), Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, fellow 1661-70, B.A. 1663 (as Mathew), M.A. 1666 (as Matthew), vicar of Merton, Oxon, 1668; perhaps rector of Alwington 1668, and of Alphington, Devon, 1677; buried 15 Aug., 1712. See Boase, 74.

Beele, Robert, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657; B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663, incorp. at Cambridge 1664, vicar of St. Stephen-juxta-Saltash 1666, rector of Dittisham, Devon, 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Sommers, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664; vicar of Almondsbury, co. Gloucester, 1665, rector of Roborough 1671, and of Marwood, Devon, 1673. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stephens, Henry, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, B.A. 28 Feb., 1659-60, M.A. 1662, vicar of Overton, Wilts, 1668; father of George 1699, of Henry 1686, and of John 1695. See St. Paul's S. R. 47.
Hunkyn, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657; student of Middle Temple 1658, as eldest son of Joseph, of Lifton, Devon, esq.; buried there 29 Feb., 1688-9. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rowe, Edward, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, B.A. 1660, M.A. 1663; one of these names vicar of Morden with rectory of Charlborough, Dorset, 1663-95. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pridham, Edward, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8, B.A. 1661; M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1665, rector of Morchard Bishops, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Drake, Dennis, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. Michaelmas Term 1657; B.A. 1661, M.A. 1664, (son of Thomas, of Wiscomb), of Churchstanton, in holy orders, father of Thomas 1702.
Haydon, Gideon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, of Cadhay, Devon (son and heir of Gedeon); buried at Ottery St. Mary, 2 March, 1702-3; father of Gideon 1684.
Hippisley, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8; one of these names rector of Meesden, Herts, 1675; see also Thomas 1652. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Nicholls, Peter, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1660, rector of Kelly, Devon, 1662, as Nicholl. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gregor, Francis, of Exeter Coll. arm., matric. 15 June, 1657, student of Inner Temple, 1660, as of Trewarthennick, Cornwall. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Chudleigh, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, eldest son of Sir George, 2nd bart., and brother of George 1654, and of William 1647.
Wolrich, John (Woolrich), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8; rector of Little Wenlock, Salop, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Elford, Walter, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8; perhaps son of William of Bickleigh, and brother of Francis 1660.
Elmeston, James, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, rector of Harford, Devon, 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hyde, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657, B.A. 1661; M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1668.
Williams, Richard, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8.
Stephens, William, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657.
Tyse, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8.
Reynolds, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657.
Rice, Ephraim, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8.
Wightman, William, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657.
Rose, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1657-8.
Penros, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 June, 1657.

Grenvil, Dennis, equitis fil. nat. min. Exeter Coll., 20 matric. 6 Aug., 1658, M.A. 28 Sept., 1660, created D.D. Dec., 1670; (s. Sir Bevil, of Kilkhampton, Cornwall, kt.), rector of Kilkhampton 1661, and of Easington and Elwick, co. Durham, 1664, and of Sedgfield 1668, archdeacon and canon of Durham 1662; fellow of Eton College 1662, chaplain in ordinary to Charles II., dean of Durham 1684 (a nonjuror), refused to take the oath of allegiance to William and Mary, and was deprived of all his preferments 1 Feb., 1690-1; died in Paris 7 (or 18) April, 1703. See Ath. iv. 497; Fasti, ii. 229; Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 141; Rawl. ii. 120, xvii. 186; & D.N.B.

Carswell, Francis, pleb. (altered to arm.). Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658; B.A. 1660, M.A. 19 Jan., 1663-4, B. & D.D. 1681, vicar of Bray, Berks, chaplain to Charles II., etc., died 24 Aug., 1709, described by Hearne as an old, rich, stingy turncoat, hated in his parish. See Hearne, i. 73, 302, ii. 24; & Fasti, ii. 381.

Morice, Nicholas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658, B.A. 1660; B.C.L. from All Souls' Coll. 1666, student of Inner Temple 1659, as of Werrington, Devon (s. Sir William), M.P. Newport (Cornwall), March, 1667-78; brother of William 1647. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

St. John, Oliver, arm. fil. nat. max. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658 (possibly 2nd son of Henry, of Farley), M.P. Stockbridge in 4 parliaments 1678-9, 1679-81, 1681, 1689, in which year he died; father of Oliver 1695. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Porter, Peter, gent. (altered to "arm."). Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658, B.A. 1660; student of Inner Temple 1658 (as s. Roger, of Trematon, Cornwall, gent.); buried in the Temple church Tuesday night, 16 July, 1661. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Almont, Roger, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 23 March, 1658-9; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 24 Feb., 1662-3, M.A. 1666, B.D. 1677, died 31 Aug., 1710, aged 67, monumental inscription Trinity College, will proved (Oxford), 16 Dec., 1720. See Gutch, i. 533.

Mallack, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658; (s. John), baptised at Axmouth 20 Jan., 1638-9, vicar of St. Pancras, Devon, 1665, and rector of Farway. Devon, 1668; died 1693; brother of John 1657. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lawrence, Richard, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9, B.A. 1662; one of these names rector of Preston Bagot, co. Warwick, 1677, and rector of Tarrant Rawston, Dorset, 1688; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

L'Isle, Jos. (Josua in M.R.), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1661 (as Joshua), vicar of Little Clacton 1665-1712 (as M.A.) and of Thorpe-in-le-Soken, Essex, 1666-1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gardiner, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658; possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1658, as son and heir of Henry, late of Jenningsbury, Herts, esq. See Clutterbuck, ii. 183; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Saunders, Humphrey, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9, B.A. 1662; M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1665, vicar of Netherbury, Dorset, 1667; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Moyle, Joseph, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9 (perhaps s. John, 1608), and baptised 20 Nov., 1636; buried at St. Germans 17 May, 1701; brother of Walter 1650. See Vivian's Cornish Visitations.
Langford, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1661, as son and heir of Charles, of Sibston Castle, Salop, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Clare, William, 'cler. fil.' Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1658; B.A. 1662, rector of Clyst St. George, 1664, father of George 1699, and of Ambrose 1691. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hawkey, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658, B.A. 1662, fellow 1663-8, M.A. 1665, rector of Marhamchurch, Cornwall, 1673-1728. See Boase, 75; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Beauchamp, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658; probably of Trevince, Cornwall (son of John), buried at Gwennap 20 Dec., 1693. See Vivian's Visitations of Cornwall.
Watts, Goodwin, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658; student of Lincoln's Inn 1658, as son and heir of Richard, of Guildford, Surrey, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Davy, Robert, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 1662, perhaps bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1673. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Baron, Christopher, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658, a student of the Inner Temple 1658, as of Egloskerry, Cornwall, gent. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Walter, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1659, as of London gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hooper, Edmond, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658; B.A. 1661, rector of Petrockstow, Devon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sa(u)nders, Solomon, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 9 July, 1658; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 11 March, 1661-2.
Hockmore, Charles, of Exeter Coll., arm. matric. 11 Dec., 1658 (s. Gregory), brother of the next-named.
Hockmore, (Sir) Gregory, of Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658 (s. Gregory); knighted 5 March, 1675-6.
Hunkyn, Joseph, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658; perhaps brother of the last-named.
Hayne, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 July, 1658, christian name "Jos." in Mat. Reg.
Cowling, William, 'cler. fil.' Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658, B.A. 1662.
Stephens, William (Steephens), "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Hill, John, arm. fil. nat. max. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Toms, William, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 March, 1658-9.
Barrell, Bartholomew, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658.
Evans, William (Evens), arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Yeo, William, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9.
Cornish, Philip, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9.
Stephens, Henry, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 March, 1658-9.
Wood(h)all, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Holcombe, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Smith, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Watts, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Woodall, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Verman, Samuel, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Dawson, Robert, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Edwards, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Mills, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.
Ewens, Ralph, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Smith, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1658.
Grundy, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1658.

Elwill, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 March, 1659; Sir John Elwill, of Exeter, merchant, bred at the university and designed by his father to be a parson, receiver-general for Devon several years, Le Neve; knighted at Kensington 28 April, 1696, created a baronet 25 Aug., 1709, M.P. Bearalston 1681, 1689-90, 1695-8; died 25 April, 1717. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Goold, Henry, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1659; possibly bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1667 (as son and heir of Andrew, of Winsham, Somerset, gent.), serjeant-at-law 1692, king's serjeant 1693 and knighted, a judge of king's bench 1699 until his death 26 March, 1710; father of Davidge. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Trenchard, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll. 19 May, 1659, not matriculated; possibly student of Middle Temple 1659, as of Woolverton, Dorset, esq. (s. Thomas), M.P. Poole Nov., 1670, until his death in 1672; brother of John 1665, and of Robert 1662. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Newcourt, Tobias, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 May, 1659; student of Middle Temple 1661 (as son and heir of John, late of Pickwell, Devon, esq., deceased); buried in the Temple church Tuesday, 10 Sept., 1661. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Nosworthy, Edward, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1673 (as son and heir of Edward, of St. Ives, Cornwall. esq.), M.P. St. Ives 1678-81; died in 1701. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Smith, Philip, arm. fil. nat. max. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1658, as son and heir of Philip, who was son of Henry, of Boydon, Wilts. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gregory, Anthony, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659, B.A. 17 Jan., 1662-3, M.A. 1665, rector of West Putford, Devon, 1668, and of Merton, Devon, 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hele, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659; possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1664, as son and heir of Warwick, of Teynton Regis, Devon, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Paull, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 May, 1659; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1663, rector of Staunton Prior 1669, and of Timsbury, Somerset, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stafford, Hugh, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 March, 1659; student of Middle Temple 1660, as son and heir of Hugh, of Dowland, Devon, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bosavern, Walter, (ser.) Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659; B.A. 1662, son of Martin, baptized at Madron, Cornwall, 28 July, 1639. See Vivian's Visitations of Cornwall.
Reed, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 March, 1659, B.A. 1662; one of these names vicar of Littleham, Devon, 1666. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hatt, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 May, 1659, B.A. 17 Jan., 1662-3, student of Inner Temple 1660, as of London. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Daye, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 March, 1659; B.A. 1662 (as Day), rector of St. Just, Cornwall, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Elston, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659; B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666, rector of Langtree, Devon, 1667. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Osborne, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659, B.A. 1663; perhaps vicar of Liskeard, Cornwall, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Floyer, Anthony, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1659; perhaps of Abbots Wooton, Dorset (son of William), and father of William 1708.
Colwell, Robert, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659, vicar of Dean Prior, Devon, 1674. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wither, John, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659, B.A. 17 Jan., 1662-3; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1667.
Flower, William, "serv." Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659, B.A. 1662; M.A. from Hart Hall 30 March, 1667.
King, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 17 Jan., 1662-3.
Ceely, William, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 25 March, 1659, perhaps brother of John 1658.
Hele, Walter, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659; probably son of Matthew 1634.
Fortescue, Edward, arm. fil. nat. max. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 May, 1659.
Sydenham, Humphrey, "serv." Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1659, B.A. 1663.
Blackaller, Henry, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 May, 1659.
Markes, Robert, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 25 March, 1659.
Webb, Stephen, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 2 May, 1659.
Harris, Edward, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659.
Hall, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1659.
Martyn, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 2 May, 1659.
Powell, Thomas, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 May, 1659.
Saffin, John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1659.

Treby, (Sir) George, Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660 (as arm.); bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1671, bencher 1681 (as son and heir of Peter, of Plympton, Devon, gent.), recorder of Plympton 1678, and of London 1680-5 and 1688-92, and knighted 20 Jan., 1680-1, solicitor-general 1689, attorney-general 1689, serjeant-at-law 1692, chief justice common pleas 1692-1700, M.P. Plympton in 6 parliaments (March) 1677-81, and 1689-92; died 13 Dec., 1700, buried in the Temple church 16th; father of the next. See Ath. iv. 499; Foster's Judges and Barristers; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Clavering, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660; of Whitehouse, co. Durham, admitted to Gray's Inn 1661 (as son and heir of James, of Axwell, co. Durham, arm.), died in his father's lifetime 26 Feb., 1687-8, perhaps father of James 1687. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Glynn, John, of Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660; possibly student of Middle Temple 1664 (as 2nd son of William, of Glynne, Cornwall, esq., deceased), buried in the Temple church 4 April, 1689; admon. 20 May at Exeter. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Mill, Hugh, s. Hugh, of West Putford, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1660, aged 20; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 1664; rector of Beaford, Devon, 1666; father of the next; his father rector of West Putford 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bond, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1663, M.A. from Exeter Coll. 1666, one of these names rector of Kirtlington, Oxon, 1666, and of Wendlebury 1666. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lobb, George, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1667; rector of Nymet Rowland, Devon, 1665, and vicar of Milton Abbot, Devon, 1668, See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bestwick, Richard, 'serv.' Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663; M.A. from New Coll. 1666, fellow of Exeter Coll. 1667-71, vicar of Westport Wilts, 1670. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Boase, 76.

Heart, Abel, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, rector of Rollstone, Wilts, 1666, and rector of Cranham, co. Gloucester, 1677, as Hart. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Robinson, Richard, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1660, as son and heir of Lionel, barrister of that society. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hickes, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, rector of Cardinham, Cornwall, 1671; perhaps father of Stephen 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Potbury, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; B.A. 1664, vicar of Payhembury, Devon, 1666; father of John and William. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Trigg(s), Robert, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660 (as Trigge); B.A. 1663; rector of Lamorran, Cornwall, 1663; father of Robert 1689. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tanton, Jos(eph), pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663; vicar of Quethiocke, Cornwall 1673, as Taunton; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lewis, William (Lewse), pleb., subs. as of Somerset. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660, vicar of Pitminster, Somerset, 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Pearse, Edmond, " ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660; B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666, vicar of Staverton, Devon, 1671; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Downe, Nicholas, 'serviens.' Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; one of these names vicar of St. Bruard, Cornwall, 1691. See Foster's Index Ecc.
Denbaude, William, 'serviens.' Exeter Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660; B.A. 1663, rector of Oakford, Devon, 1689. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Archer, Edward, 'cler. fil.' Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, B.A. 1664, vicar of Manaccan, Cornwall, 1666. Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Walker, John, cler. fil. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, demy Magdalen Coll. 1662-7, B.A. 1664, M.A. 5 Feb., 1666-7. See Bloxam, v. 252.
Hals, Edmund, s. Matthew, of Efford, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1660, aged 18; died 17 March, 1678-9; brother of Peter 1669.
Hooke, Samuel, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, B.A. 1664, vicar of Minehead, Somerset, 1666, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Trewinnard, Joseph, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660; rector of St. Mary Weeke, Cornwall, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edbrooke, William, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660; vicar of Sidmouth, Devon, 1664. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Andrewe, Thomas, 'serv.' Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660; B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666, admon. at Oxford 23 Aug., 1673.
Byle, William, 'serviens,' of Cornwall. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660; B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667.
Davy, Alexander, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1663.
Whiteway, Edward, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 April, 1660, B.A. 1663, M.A. 1666.
Burges, Thomas, of Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660; B.A. 1664.
Bawden, Reinold, of Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660.
Buckland, Samuel, 'ser.,' of Devon. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660.
Quicke, Thomas, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660, B.A. 1664.
Parr, Christopher, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1660.
Stoforde, Richard, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.
Harris, Thomas, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.
Hooper, William, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 6 June, 1660.
Goold, James, "ser." Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.
Turney, Henry, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.
Kestell, John, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1660.

Snell, George, s. John, of Thurlstone, Devon, minister, Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 17, fellow 1662-71, B.A. 1665, M.A. 1668; vicar of Menheniott, Cornwall, 1670, rector of Thurlstone, Devon, 1679, and prebendary of Carswill, or Kerswell (in Castro Exon), 1679, canon of Exeter 1685-1700, archdeacon of Totnes 1694, until his death 14 Jan., 1700-1; brother of Thomas 1662. See Boase, 74; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Nickolls, Matthew, "ser.," s. Matthew, of Fritherell Stoke, Devon, pleb., p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1661, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1664 (as Nickolls), vicar of Burrington, Devon, 1666, prebendary of Lower Hayne, and of Deans in collegiate church of Chumleigh, Devon, 1677, rector of Eggesford, Devon, 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gostwyke, William, s. Gabriel, of North Tawton, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; B.A. from Hart Hall, 1664, M.A. from Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1669; incorporated 13 July, 1669, rector of Purley, Berks, 1684. See Ath. iv. 500; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Norris, Samuel, s. John, of Alborne, Wilts, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 5 April, 1661, aged 17; fellow 1663-82, B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, proctor 1679, B.D. 1680, rector of Tilehurst, Berks, 1681, and of Englefield, Berks, 1708; brother of John 1676. See Boase, 75; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rowe, John, s. Nicholas, of Lamerton, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 15, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1674; licenced 5 May, 1687 (widower) to marry Jane, daughter of Sir William Courtenay, of Powderham Castle, Devon, bart. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Hu(t)chings, Richard, s. R(ichard), of Carway, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 16; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, B.D. 1680, fellow 1663 until his death 2 April, 1718; will proved 16 April; a benefactor to his college. See Gutch, i. 120; & Boase, 75.

Prideaux, John, s. Edmund, of Padstow, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18; of Prideaux Place, Cornwall, student of Middle Temple 1664; buried at Padstow, 2 Dec., 1704; brother of Humphrey 1668, and Nicholas. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Bird, Bernard, (ser.), s. John, of Coomb Rawly, Devon, pleb. p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1664 (as Byrd), vicar of Combe, Hants, 1666, and of Ufculme, Devon, 1686. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rossington, James, s. James, of St. Allen, Cornwall, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18, B.A. 1665; vicar of Up Ottery, Devon, 1667-1702, and rector of Lezant, Cornwall, 1676-1702; brother of Anthony. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hurrell, Thomas, s. John, of Hendham, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, fellow 1671-3; rector of Beer Ferrers, Devon, 1673, until his death 1721. See Boase, 77; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Gay, John, s. John, of Frithlestock, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 20; B.A. 1664; baptised at Barnstaple 24 Nov., 1639; buried at Frithelstock 25 Jan., 1716-17; father of Richard. See Calamy, i. 229.

Wyatt, Robert, s. Henry, of Taunton, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 8 March, 1661-2, aged 21; B.A. from New Inn Hall, 1665; rector of Chipstable, Somerset, 1670. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Elford, Hugh, s. H(ugh), of Woodley, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; brother of Walter 1664; his father vicar of Walkhampton, Devon, 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Flood, John, s. John, of Sarum town, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1661, aged 15; B.A. 23 Jan., 1665-6, vicar of Cricklade, Wilts, 1668; father of John 1686. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Webber, Humphrey, s. Galfr., of Barstable, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 20, B.A. 1664; rector of Challacombe Rawleigh, Devon, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wolcot, Maximilian (Wollcott), s. William, of Sidbury, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 19, B.A. 1665; rector of Feniton, Devon, 1690. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Karslake, Henry, s. Roger, of Nympton St. George, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18; rector of Beaworthy, Devon, 1665, as Kerslake. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Periam, John (Peryam), "ser.," s. J., of St. Mary Ottery, Devon, pleb., p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 May, 1661, aged 18; rector of Mamhead, Devon, 1665. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Batt, Richard, s. Richard, of East Chinnocke, Somerset, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1661, aged 21; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1668. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Wyatt, Richard, "ser." s. R., of Marlborough, Wilts, pleb. p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; vicar of Uphaven, Wilts, 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Major, John, "ser.," s. John, of Ashburton, Devon, pleb. pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1661, aged 17; vicar of Cadbury, Devon, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Walrond, Henry, s. William, of Bradfield, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1662. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Heart, William, s. William, of Foxley, Wilts, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 6 April, 1661, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1664-7, B.A. 1665. See Bloxam, v. 271.
Priaulx, William (Pryaulx), s. Peter, of Guernsey ("Anglo-Sarniensis"), arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 5 May, 1661, aged 17; fellow 1661-2. See Boase, 74.
Lloyd, John, "ser.," s. William, of London, pleb., pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 May, 1661, aged 19; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 18 Jan., 1664-5.
Hart, Thomas, s. Robert, of Gloucester city, minister, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 16; (subs.) "ser." B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667.
Hancock, John, s. John, of Comb Martin, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 May, 1661, aged 18; brother of Edward 1665.
Salmon, Henry, s. H., of Stretton, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 17; father of Henry 1707.
Carslake, Richard, 'ser.,' s. Richard, of Sidbury, Devon, pleb., p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 5 April, 1661, aged 18.
Read, John, s. Christopher, of Saltash, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18; B.A. 1664.
Acland, John, s. James, of Exeter, Devon, pleb. p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18; B.A. 1665.
Harris, Maurice, s. John, of Radford, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 6 April, 1661, aged 16; B.C.L. 1668.
Horder, Moses, s. Richard, of Kineton, Dorset, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 8 March, 1661-2, aged 18.
Hicks, John, s. J., of Alternon, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1661, aged 18.
Dix, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Exeter, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 May, 1661, aged 18.

Snell, Thomas, s. John, of Thurlestone, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 June, 1662, aged 16, B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669; (incorporated at Cambridge 1675), B.D. from All Soul's Coll. 1676; canon of Exeter 1679, vicar of Bampton, Oxon, 1677, and prebendary of Kerswell in castro Exon, 1701; his father rector of Thurlestone, 1635, and of Lezant, Cornwall, 1638, and prebendary of Kerswell in castro Exon 1660; brother of George 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pendarves, Thomas, s. Ric., of Pendarves, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 16; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669; student of Middle Temple 1664, vicar of St. Govan 1682, rector of Mawgan 1683, and of St. Columb Major (all Cornwall) 1695; baptised at Camborne 4 Sept., 1645; died 16 March, 1703, and buried at St. Columb Major 18th. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Burgh, Edward, s. Edward, of Preston, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 June, 1662, aged 17, B.A. 1666; M.A. from New Coll. 21 Jan., 1668-9; fellow Exeter Coll. 1670-8, canon of Wells 1670, rector of Stoke Gifford 1672, of Monksilver 1675, of Sutton Bingham, Somerset, 1676, rector of Melbury Bubb, Dorset, 1677. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Boase, 76.

Crispe, William, s. John, of Chipping Norton, Oxon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1662, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1674, brother of John same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers. [Addenda: licenced (v.g.) 10 Nov., 1681, to marry Judith Winston, of Everdon, Northants, spinster, aged 25.]

Clobery, John, s. Christopher, of Bradstone, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 18; of Bradstone, admitted to the Inner Temple 13 Feb., 1662-3, M.P. Truro 1695-8, buried in Bradstone Church 23 March, 1698-9, brother of Christopher same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Vivian, Thomas, s. John, of Colomb Major, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 17; of Trewan, Cornwall, baptised 13 Nov., 1645, student of Inner Temple 1662; perhaps M.P. Fowey 1695-1700; buried at St. Columb Major 8 June, 1716. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Crispe, John, s. John, of Chipping Norton, Oxon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1668, dead before 1 May, 1700, made his will 19 April, 1699, proved 29 Nov. following, brother of William same date, See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Polwhele, Thomas, s. John, of Treworgan, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1662, aged 17; fellow 1664-78, B.A. 1667, M.A. 1671, vicar of Newlyn East, Cornwall, 1677, until deprived 1690, as a Jacobite; brother of John 1658, See Boase, 76; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Kellond, Samuel, s. John, of Paynsford, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 17; baptised at Totnes 11 Sept., 1643; student of Middle Temple 1664; vicar of Paington, Devon, 1669; brother of John 1652. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Fox, Obadiah, s. Thomas, of Davidstowe, Cornwall, pleb. pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 June, 1662, aged 17; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1666; M.A. from Exeter Coll. 1669, incorporated at Cambridge 1671, rector of Stockleigh Pomeroy, Devon, 1668. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Vivian, Richard, s. Richard, of St. Ervan, Cornwall, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 18 (subscribes "servitor"), B.A. 1666; rector of St. Ervan 1666, until his death in 1708; father of John 1687, and of William 1702. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bastard, Samson, s. William, of Garstone, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 17; B.A. 1667, fellow 1664-70, M.A. 1670, rector of South Pool, Devon, 1668, until his death 12 Sept., 1676. See Boase, 76; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Burford, John, s. Ed., of Yeovil, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 19; scholar of Wadham Coll. 1663-76, B.A. 1666, M.A. 18 Feb., 1668-9, rector of Monksilver, Somerset, 1677. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Gardiner, 245.

Simeon, Richard, s. Edm., of Sidenton, co. Gloucester, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 17, B.A. 1666; vicar of Beenham 1686, and of Bucklebury, Berks, 1686; father of Edmund, Richard, and Daniel. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Russell, Richard, s. Richard, of Penton, Southants, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 May, 1662, aged 15; brother of Henry 1674; his father rector of Penton Mewsey 1636, and perhaps rector of Brixton, Isle of Wight, 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Clobery, Christopher, s. Christopher, of Bradstone, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 17; of the Inner Temple 13 Feb. preceding, brother of John same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Jacob, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Salisbury, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1662, aged 15; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, vicar of Enford, Wilts, 1670, of Collingbourne Kingston, Wilts, 1676. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fowler, Richard, s. Rich., of Crewkerne, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 18; B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667, vicar of Swell, 1668, and of Fifehead, Somerset, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pinsent, John, s. Edw., of Bovey-Tracey, Devon, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 16; B.A. 1667, M.A. 1672, vicar of Coleshill, Berks, 1667. See Rawl. vi. 87; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rashleigh, Philip, s. John (George in Mat. Reg.), of Coombe, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 18; a captain; baptised at Fowey 19 Nov., 1643, buried there 24 Feb., 1672.
Curgenven, Thomas, 'serviens,' s. Rich., of Leland, Cornwall, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 18; rector of Orcheston St. George, Wilts, 1690. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Gubbes, Thomas, s. Anthony, of Penzance, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 19; B.A. 19 June, 1666, vicar of Fisherton Delamere, Wilts, 1673. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jennings, Daniel, s. George, of Gerrans, Cornwall, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 December, 1662, aged 18; one of these names vicar of Welham, co. Leicester, 1679. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Fryer, Joseph, s. Francis, of Exeter, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 17; B.A. 1666, rector of Woodmansterne, Surrey, 1673. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tonkin, William, s. Ric., of Mullion, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 18; vicar of St. Mullion 1683. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Harris, Humphrey, s. John, of St. Issie, Cornwall, pleb. pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 19; B.A. 9 Feb., 1665-6; brother of Joseph 1670.
Hippisley, Richard, s. Richard, of Lamborne, Berks, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 May, 1662, aged 17; died s.p.; brother of John 1664.
Bartlett, Ellis, s. Ellis, of Brandescombe, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 17.
Leigh, Peter, s. Peter, of Egloskerry, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 June, 1662, aged 18.
Pickard, Edmund, s. Edmund, of North Ham, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 28 June, 1662, aged 17.
Hortop, John, s. Rob., of West Putford, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1662, aged 22.
Jordan, William, "ser.," s. William, of Exeter, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 16.
Sture, John, s. Philip, of Bradley, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 April, 1662, aged 18.
Scawen, Richard, of Treharne, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 19.

Drewe, Edward, s. Franc., of Grange, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1663, aged 18, B.A. 1667; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 1670, rector of Lezant, Cornwall, 1670, and of Brideston, Devon, 1670, canon of Exeter 1671, archdeacon of Cornwall 1672, and chancellor of Exeter 1675, until his death 17 Dec., 1714, aged 70, buried in Exeter Cathedral 21st, brother of Thomas 1652, and father of Francis 1690. See Fasti, ii. 319; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rashleigh, John, s. John, of Menabilly, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1663, aged 18; of Corner Hall, Herts; baptised 20 Nov., 1644; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1672; licenced 2 Aug., 1671, to marry Mary, daughter of Hugh Gamlin, of St. Clement Danes, Middlesex, gent.; brother of Jonathan 1659, and of Philip 1663. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

St. Aubyn, (Sir) John, (Seynt Aubyn), s. John, of Clowance, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663; baptised at St. Andrew's, Plymouth, 6 April, 1645, created a baronet 11 Dec., 1671, M.P. St. Michael 1678-9, 1679-81, Helston 1689-90, and 1690-5, buried at Crowan 24 Jan., 1699; brother of Guy and Thomas 1667. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Drake, (Sir) Francis, s. Thomas, of Buckland Monachorum, Devon, bart. (sic.). Exeter Coll., matric. 3 June, 1663, aged 16; created M.A. 28 Sept., 1663, 3rd baronet, M.P. Buckland 1673-81, 1681, 1689-90, Tavistock 1690-5, Nov., 1696-1700, baptized at Buckland Monachorum 1 May, 1642, buried there 15 June, 1718. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Collier, John (Collyer), s. William, of Llanlivery, Cornwall, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18, B.A. 28 Jan., 1667-8; incorp. at Cambridge 1673, M.A. from King's Coll. 1673, vicar of Fremington, Devon, 1670, of St. Gluvias, Budock, Cornwall, 1671, and of Moorlinch, Somerset, 1671. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Downe, Thomas, s. Anth., of Northam, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1663, aged 17, as Downes, born at Barnstaple; and again 6 May, 1665, aged 19, as Downe; rector of Sutcombe, Devon, 1670, his father instituted to vicarage of Northam 19 Jan., 1640-1. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Marker, Frederick, s. Anth., of Totnes, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1663, aged 18; B.A. 1667, vicar of Stokenham, Devon, 1674; licenced 10 Feb., 1675-6, to marry Elizabeth Earle, of Totnes, spinster. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Trotman, Samuel, s. Samuel, of Bucknell, Oxon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. entry 11 Dec., 1663, aged 13; of Siston, co. Gloucester, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1671, bencher 1682, M.P. Bath Feb., 1706-7, until his death 30 Jan., 1719-20. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Rashleigh, Philip, s. John, of Menabilly, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 16; bar.-at-law of the Middle Temple 1672; buried at Fowey 17 March, 1682, unmarried; brother of John 1663, and of Jonathan 1659. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Careye, Peter, s. Peter, of St. Peter's Port, Guernsey, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 April, 1663, aged 18; B.A. 1666, fellow 1664-70, then in orders and married, rector of St. Saviour's, Guernsey, 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Boase, 75.

Ryder, Martin, s. John, of Lee, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1671, bencher 1692 (as of Bickleigh, Devon); father of John 1704, and Samuel 1709. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Sargent, Thomas, s. Degory, of Dunterton, pleb. p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1663, aged 18, B.A. 1667 (as Sarjeant); perhaps vicar of Nocton 1661-7, and of Timberland, co. Lincoln, 1669-70, as Serjeant. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Preston, William, s. Nic., of Saltash, Cornwall, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 19; perhaps rector of Ashton 1671-90, and vicar of Harpford, Devon, 1690-6; father of William 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hickes, Nicholas, s. John, of Alternon, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 16; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, rector of Cheriton Fitzpaine, Devon, 1676; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ivie, George, s. George, of Crediton, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 16; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, rector of North Bovey, Devon, 1672; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Woolcombe, Richard (Wollcom), s. William, of Bickington, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Holcombe Burnell, Devon, 1679. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ford, Roger, s. Charles, of Fordmore, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 July, 1663, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1663; bapt. at Uffculme 25 Oct., 1646. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ralph, John, s. Ralph, of Gornwall, Cornwall, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18; B.A. 1667, vicar of Sithney 1668, and of St. Erth, Cornwall, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Scott, John, s. Rog., of Devizes, Wilts, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 May, 1663, aged 18; B.A. 14 Feb., 1666-7, M.A. 1669; rector of Bishopstrow, Wilts, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Weekes, Richard, s. Rich., of Brinkworth, Wilts, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 May, 1663, aged 16; perhaps vicar of Llangunnor, co. Carnarvon, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tayler, Isaac, s. Joh., of Devizes, Wilts, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 May, 1663, aged 17, B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669; rector of Almer, Dorset, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Yeo, Thomas, s. William, of East Buckland, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Morthoe, Devon, 1693. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Herle, John, s. Edw., of Prideaux, Cornwall, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1673. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Pope, Roger, s. Rog., of Evan Hall, Salop, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18; perhaps M.P. Bridgnorth 1685-7. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Robbins, Samuel, s. John, of Exeter, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1663, aged 17; perhaps rector of Trevalga, Cornwall, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Keigwin, John, s. William, of Tretane, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 16; of Mousehole, died in 1693; perhaps father of the next-named.
Davie, John, s. John, of Sandford, Devon, bart. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 June, 1663, aged 18; buried at Sandford 11 Jan., 1667-8.
Downes, Thomas, s. Anth., of Barnstaple, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1663, aged 17. See Thomas Downe 1665.
Trevethicke, William, s. William, of Petrockstow, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 19, B.A. 1666.
Gage, John (Gayge), s. John, of Bodmin, Cornwall, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 June, 1663, aged 16; subs. "serviens."
Bond, William, s. William, of Calstock, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 16; B.A. 1666.
Randall, Abr(aham), s. William, of Berry Pomeroy, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 19.
Glanvill, Thomas, s. Rich., of Honiton, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 July, 1663, aged 18; B.A. 1667.
Cornish, William, s. John, of Ottery St. Mary, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18.
Slade, Nathaniel, s. Sam., of Otterton, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 April, 1663, aged 16.
Piggot, Lionel, s. Henry, of Dorchester, Oxon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 June, 1663, aged 19.
Jones, John, s. Th., of Offee, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 May, 1663, aged 16.

Hippisley, John, s. Richard, of Lamborne, Berks, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1667; licenced 15 Feb., 1670-1 to marry Catherine, daughter of Richard Southby, of Carswell, Berks, esq.; brother of Richard 1662, and father of Richard 1698. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Elford, Walter, s. Hugh, of Walkhamton, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 May, 1664, aged 19; rector of Woodleigh, Devon, 1676-1728; licenced (v.g.) 28 March, 1694, to marry Mary Woodleigh, of Cornworth, Devon, aged 40; brother of Hugh 1661. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Long, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Exeter (city), minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 15; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 1 Feb., 1667-8, M.A. 20 March, 1670-1, rector of Whimple, Devon, 1676, canon of Exeter 1681; brother of Nicholas same date. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Haydon, Gideon, s. Nic., of Ottery St. Mary, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 May, 1664, aged 16; B.A. 23 Jan., 1667-8, incorporated at Cambridge 1668; admitted a student of Lincoln's Inn 1666, brother of Nicholas 1668, and of Walter 1665. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Piper, Charles (Pyper), s. Tho., of Liskeard, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1664, aged 19; B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671; rector of Bradoke, Cornwall, 1672, vicar of Walkhampton, Devon, 1675, and rector of Jacobstowe, Cornwall, 1685. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Grayle, John (Graile), s. John, of Tidworth, Wilts, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1664-5, aged 16; B.A. 28 Jan., 1667-8, M.A. 1670, rector of Blickling, Norfolk, 1674, and of Waxham 1682. See Ath. iv. 501; Rawl. iii. 312; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Ernle, (Sir) John, 1s. John, of Buriton, Wilts, equitis. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1664, aged 17; entered Lincoln's Inn 1666; knighted; M. P. for Calne 1685, until he died 25 Oct., 1686; father of John K. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Drinckwater, William, s. William, of Mount Edgcomb, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 16, B.A. 1668; M.A. from Hart Hall 1671, vicar of Mevagissey, Cornwall, 1677. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Ivie, James, s. John, of Sarum, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 17; B.A. 1667, M.A. 1670; rector of Ashmore, Dorset, 1682, and of Tollard Royal, Wilts, 1697; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Holloway, William, s. Rich., of Charford, Hants, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1664-5, aged 18; B.A. from New Coll. 1668; one of these names an LL.D., rector of Great Horwood, Bucks, 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hill, Jeffrey, s. John, of Filliegh, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 17; B.A. from New Inn Hall 1667 (as Geoffrey); one of these names vicar of Minehead, Somerset, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Trevisa, John, s. William, of Crocadon, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 16, B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671; clerk in orders, baptised at St. Mellion 12 Aug., 1647; buried at Crediton 24 April, 1679.

Worth, Thomas, s. Henry, of Washfield, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 17, B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671; rector of High Bickington, Devon, 1679; perhaps father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Drake, (Sir) John, s. John, of Ash, Devon, bart. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 March, 1664-5, aged 17; 2nd baronet, of the Inner Temple 1667, born 13 Jan., 1646-7, died 13 March, 1683-4. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Enys, Samuel, s. Sa., of Penryn, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 May, 1664, aged 16; a student of the Inner Temple 1664 (as of Gluvias. Cornwall, gent.); died 2 Feb., 1672. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Northcote, William, s. John, of Hayne, Devon, bart. Exeter Coll., matric. 22 July, 1664, aged 15; baptised at Newton St. Cyres 26 Nov., 1648, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1674. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Carthew, John, s. Tho., of S. Issie, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 18, B.A. 1668; M.A. from Hart Hall 1671, rector of St. Erme, Cornwall, 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Sampson, Thomas, s. Peter, of Liskeard, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 18, B.A. 1668; vicar of Tywardreth 1677, and of St. Wenn, Cornwall, 1680. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Orchard, John, s. Tho., of Hennock, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 19; B.C.L. 1671; rector of Eaton Hastings, Berks, 1671; father of Thomas 1690. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pett, (Sir) Phineas, s. Ph., of Greenwich, Kent, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 17; a commissioner of the navy, knighted 5 Aug., 1680; died 1696. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Hawkins, Edward s. Edw, of Maristow, Devon, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1664, aged 16; B.A. 1668; perhaps rector of Tetcott, Devon, 1689, vicar of Maristow 1697. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Musgrave, George, s. Rich., of Nettlecombe, Somerset, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1664-5, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1676 father of the next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Davies, Samuel, s. William, of St. Earth, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 18, B.A. 1668; M.A. from Hart Hall 1671, rector of Ludgvan 1670. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Stephens, Thomas, s. Tho., of St. Dominick, Cornwall, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1668; perhaps rector of Meavy, Devon, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hody, Gilbert, s. Hugh, of Nethaway, Devon, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 2 April, 1664, aged 17; baptised at Cornworthy 2 Feb., 1646-7; student of Middle Temple 1664. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Tredinham, Benjamin, s. John, of Tregony, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 May, 1664, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1666; died 18 Jan., 1672. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ford, John, 1s. Rich., , of London, equitis. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1664, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1664; brother of Samuel same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Courtis, Richard, s. Rich., of Budleigh, Devon, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 17; B.A. 1668, vicar of Aylisbeare, Devon, 1671. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Clarke, Jacob, s. Henry, of Ailsbeere, Devon, pleb., p. p. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 May, 1664, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Affpuddle, Dorset, 1684. See Foster Index Ecclesiasticus.
Richards, John, s. John, of Trentishoe, Devon, minister, Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 17; perhaps rector of Kentisbury, Devon, 1670; father of Escott and John 1708.
Risdon, Henry, s. Philip, of Buckland-Brewer, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 20, B.A. 1668, rector of Coryton, Devon, 1684. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Smalbro(o)ke, John, s. Rich., of Droitwich, co. Worcester, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 17; one of these names buried in Westminster abbey 10 March, 1690-1.
Coryndon, Joseph, s. John, of Bratton Clovelly, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 18; B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671, rector of Kilkhampton, Devon, died 17 Sept., 1711.
Dell, Henry, s. Rich., of Ruan-Lanyhorne, Cornwall, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 18; rector of Ruan-Lanyhorne 1685. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Terry, Nathaniel, s. Nath., of Paignton, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 July 1664, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1668; brother of James 1678, and John 1664.
Clarke, Richard, s. George, of Tiverton, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July 1664, aged 17; B.A. 1668, of the Middle Temple 1671. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
French, Thomas, s. Tho., of Musbery, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.A. 1667, rector of Southleigh, Devon, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Oltramare, John, s. Nic., of St. John's, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 16; rector of South Pool, Devon, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Trobridge, John, s. John, of Winchford, Somerset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 13 July, 1664, aged 22; rector of Stoodleigh, Devon, 1677. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Palfrey, Thomas, s. George, of Up-Ottery, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1664, aged 16; vicar of Luppitt, Devon, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Passemere, George, s. Geo., of Exeter, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.A. 1667, vicar of Awliscombe, Devon, 1668. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knight, William, s. Rog., of Thorncomb, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 16; B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Getsius, Walter, s. Daniel, of Stoke Gabriel, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 17; B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671; brother of Daniel 1651.
Long, Nicholas, s. Thomas, of city of Exeter, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 16; brother of Thomas same date, and of Richard 1671.
Torkington, Thomas, s. Fr., of Ringmere, Devon, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 18 (subs. 'serviens'); B.A. from New Inn Hall 1668.
Smith, George, s. Geo., of Exeter, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 17, B.A. 1668; perhaps M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1678.
Browne, Richard, s. M., of Fermoy, co. Cork, Ireland, pauperis. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.A. 1667.
Studley, Elisha, s. Giles, of Broad Windsor, Dorset, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 March, 1664-5, aged 17, B.A. 1668.
Ham, William, s. John, of Halberton, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1664, aged 17; B.A. 1668, M.A. 1671.
Stockwell, Joseph, s. Joseph, of New Sarum, Wilts, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 27 Jan., 1664-5, aged 17, B.A. 1668.
Dumont, Oliver, born at 'Vitre in Britannia Minore,' s. John, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18.
Gregory, Samuel, s. Sam., of High Bray, Devon, minister. Exeter Coll., matric. 15 July, 1664, aged 17; B.A. 1668.
Hull, William, s. William, of Ottery St. Mary, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 3 March, 1664-5, aged 17; B.A. 1668.
Haweis, Reinald s. John, of Callew (Killiow), Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 17.
Smalley, Oliph. (Smaly), s. Tho., of Penryn, Cornwall, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 20 May, 1664, aged 20.
Churchill, Alexander, s. Charles, of Feniton, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18.
Creed, Peter, s. Peter, of Stoke Flemming, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 18.
Barnfield, John, s. Abr., of Buckland Bruer, Devon, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18.
Browne, John, s. John, of Aylesbeere, Devon, pleb., p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 May, 1664, aged 16.
Legg, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Caerwent, co. Monmouth, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1664, aged 16.
Coode, John, s. John, of Penryn, Cornwall, pleb., p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 May, 1664, aged 16.
Strode, George, s. Rob., of Netherbury, Dorset, gent. Exeter Coll., matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18.
Sparke, William, s. Jonath., of Plymouth, arm. Exeter Coll., matric. 10 March, 1664-5, aged 19.
Moore, John, s. John, of Helston, Cornwall, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 24 May, 1664, aged 16.
Ford, Samuel, y.s. Rich, of London, equitis. Exeter Coll., matric. 26 July, 1664, aged 16.

Holdsworth, Thomas, s. John, of Modbury, Devon, pp. Exeter Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1665, aged 16; B.A. 1669, M.A. 1672, rector of North Stoneham, Hants, 1672, and of Itchen Abbots, Hants, 1691; father of the next. See Ath. iv. 501; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Clarke, Henry, s. James, of Tiverton, Devon, agricola. Exeter Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1665, aged 17; B. A. 1669, rector of Pendomer, Somerset, 1675, and of Ilminster 1683. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rowe, William, s. William, of Torrington, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 April, 1665, aged 17; perhaps rector of Tetcott 1683, and vicar of Otterton, Devon, 1689. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Samon, William (Sammon), s. John, of "Lawn S. Colomb," Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 14 July, 1665, aged 20; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 1669; M.A. from Hart Hall 1672.
Hooper, Edward, s. Edw., of Plymouth, Devon, pleb. Exeter Coll., matric. 7 July, 1665, aged 18; bar.at-law, Middle Temple, 1674. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Borough, Philip (Borrough), s. Philip, of Colyton, Devon, p.p. Exeter Coll., matric. 21 April, 1665, aged 17; B.A. 11 Feb., 1668-9.
Skinner, John, s. Arth., of Plymouth, Devon, "generosi pps." Exeter Coll., matric. 21 April, 1665, aged 15, B.A. 11 Feb., 1668-9.
